summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships persistent clipboard-hijacking wallet stealer
details: Package name @jaggle/resizeobserves is a homoglyph+pluralization typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer (~3M weekly downloads), and the README is copy-pasted from the legitimate package. The actual shipped payload is unrelated to ResizeObserver: a Python 'clipboard_guardian' module bundled in the tarball is installed and persisted by scripts.postinstall (npm-install.cjs). The postinstall escalates privileges via pkexec/sudo to apt-get/pacman/dnf install xclip/wl-clipboard, downloads https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py to /tmp and runs it under python3, then pip-installs the bundled package with --break-system-packages. It then registers persistence under deliberately deceptive names: a systemd user unit 'python3-dbus-helper.service', a macOS LaunchAgent 'com.apple.python.runtime.plist', and a Windows scheduled task 'PyRuntimeBroker', so the daemon autostarts on every login. The Python daemon (clipboard_guardian/guardian.py) polls the clipboard every ~0.4s, regex-matches cryptocurrency addresses across 35+ chains (BTC/ETH/XMR/SOL/TRX/TON/...), and silently rewrites them to attacker-controlled receive addresses hardcoded in both npm-install.cjs (MY_WALLETS, e.g. ethereum 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, bitcoin bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y) and guardian.py, with config also seeded into ~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json. Defensive evasion is built in: setproctitle disguises the process as 'python3-dbus-helper' / 'com.apple.python.runtime' / 'Python Runtime Broker', and psutil enumerates running processes for taskmgr/procmon/htop/btop/gnome-system-monitor to detect inspection. Any developer who installs this and later copies a wallet address on the same machine will silently send funds to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
